name: Backtest Engine
strategy_version: ref_bt_0001 (reference engine self-test only; real candidates are Phase 5)
scope_implemented: Event-based backtest engine (Section 19): src/backtest/engine.py walks one chronological bar grid; per bar it charges funding on open positions, manages stops/take-profits/time-stops against the bar's own high/low, then opens positions from signals decided on the PREVIOUS bar's close. Look-ahead prevention is STRUCTURAL: a signal for bar k (decision_ts=(k+1)*iv) can only fill at bar k+1's open, so no decision reads its own close or any future bar.

Fee/slippage/funding cost models tied to VERIFIED metadata (src/backtest/costs.py): maker/taker fees from configs/metadata.yaml ([VERIFIED], META gate); slippage = half-spread + notional-vs-liquidity impact, always adverse to the taker; funding charged to open positions at each funding timestamp (longs pay positive funding). fee_/slippage_/funding_multiplier are the single stress knobs.

Risk simulation (src/backtest/risk.py, Section 17): per-trade sizing size=(equity*risk_pct)/|entry-stop|, leverage-as-consequence cap, metadata min-notional/min-order-size/lot-step gate; rejects candidates the real risk manager would (portfolio heat/net-beta caps deliberately deferred to the Phase 6 risk manager, not faked).

Execution simulation + rejected-candidate logging (Section 18/19): next-bar-open fills, taker entry/exit with adverse slippage, toxic-spread + slippage-estimate hard-blockers, concurrency caps; every blocked candidate logged with reason (src/backtest/engine.py RejectedCandidate).

Report generator (src/backtest/metrics.py): all Section 19 required outputs - total return, net PnL, expectancy (R), profit factor, max drawdown, trade count; symbol/strategy/regime/session/long-short breakdowns; cost/slippage/funding breakdowns; rejected-candidate breakdown; worst trades; stability metrics. Capital-agnostic (returns + R-multiples).

Integrity guards (src/backtest/guards.py, Section 19): noise_expectancy runs the SAME engine on a structureless series and requires ~0 expectancy (engine-level look-ahead/leakage guard); future_universe_violations proves no symbol is traded before its activation_ts (survivorship/future-universe guard; SOL activates partway through to exercise it).

Walk-forward harness (src/backtest/walkforward.py, Section 16): >=K disjoint time-ordered OOS folds judged against up-front kill-criteria, plus a LOCKED hold-out evaluated exactly once; passes only when >=min_folds_passed folds clear all kill-criteria AND the hold-out is positive net of costs.
Fee/slippage stress runners (src/backtest/stress.py, Section 16): re-run the same backtest with x2 fees / +50% slippage and require the edge survives net of costs.

Deterministic reference fixture (src/backtest/reference.py): offline, reproducible series with a planted causal momentum edge (edge=trend) and a no-structure mode (edge=noise) for the look-ahead guard; reused via build_reference_inputs (memoized) through the single feature pipeline (Parity Rule).

Config + persistence: configs/backtest.yaml (backtest_version bt_0001 + cost/fold/kill-criteria/stress knobs, all version-bumpable); BacktestRun relational index + Alembic migration 0004 (idempotent create_all checkfirst); full reports written to the regenerable reports lake as JSON.
Gate checks wired into the Gate Runner: BT, WF, FEE, SLIP (src/gates/checks.py) + dependency graph in configs/gates.yaml (BT<-DATA-COV,DQ,UNIV,META,FEAT; WF<-BT; FEE/SLIP<-BT,WF).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Event-based engine with fee/slippage/funding models tied to verified metadata; rejected-candidate logging; risk+execution simulation; report generator
acceptance_criteria.evidence: src/backtest/{engine,costs,risk,metrics}.py; BT gate PASS (engine_completes=306 trades/2678 rejected, realistic_costs_modeled fees=20020.85 slippage=6308.44 funding=-97.06, fees_from_verified_metadata, all_required_outputs_present=17 metrics); tests/test_backtest.py (cost adverse-to-taker, Section 17 sizing identity, leverage cap, min-notional gate, costs charged per trade, rejected-candidate logging, all required outputs).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Look-ahead / survivorship / future-universe guards enforced and tested
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Structural next-bar-open fill asserted in tests/test_backtest.py::test_engine_fills_signal_at_next_bar_open_not_own_close; noise series ~0 expectancy (BT no_lookahead_noise: expectancy_r=-0.1059 <= 0.05); future_universe_violations == [] with a late-activating symbol exercised (BT no_future_universe_leakage). docs/decisions/0005.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Gates PASS: BT, WF, FEE, SLIP
acceptance_criteria.evidence: uv run python -m src.gates.runner --gate {BT|WF|FEE|SLIP} -> PASS; WF 5/5 folds clear kill-criteria (need 4), locked hold-out expectancy_r=0.252 net_pnl=9753.33 evaluated once; FEE x2 -> expectancy_r=0.120 net_pnl=20069.26 survives; SLIP +50% -> expectancy_r=0.213 net_pnl=38324.11 survives; reports/phase_4/orchestrator_gate_results.json; tests/test_phase4_gates.py.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: New/changed code has tests; pytest, ruff, mypy green; coverage thresholds met on critical modules
acceptance_criteria.evidence: 136 passed (was 101); 32 new tests (tests/test_backtest.py 29 offline engine/cost/risk/guard/wf/stress tests + tests/test_phase4_gates.py 3 gate-integration tests/7 cases). ruff check + format clean (90 files); mypy Success (66 source files). Risk simulation, cost models, engine, guards, walk-forward and stress covered by direct unit tests.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: No forbidden shortcut; no live-mode default; no secrets committed (Appendix B.17 / Section 30)
acceptance_criteria.evidence: No live trading path; reference series is a labelled offline fixture never used for a live decision; runs are versioned (backtest_runs + content-addressed run_id), full reports in the git-ignored regenerable reports lake; TRADING_MODE=PAPER + live disabled unchanged; no keys added; vectorized shortcut avoided (engine is strictly event-based).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All new runtime behavior is config-driven and versioned (Section 4)
acceptance_criteria.evidence: configs/backtest.yaml pins backtest_version bt_0001 and every cost assumption, fold layout, kill-criterion and stress multiplier; with_cost_overrides keeps stress runs on the same code path; BacktestRun rows carry related_versions; reports embed settings.versions().

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Completion Report filled; docs/decisions updated for assumptions

updated_test_files: tests/test_gates.py (BT/WF now PASS as of Phase 4; the dependency-blocking + remediation invariants are now exercised via RISK<-SETUP, still NOT_RUN until Phase 5)
tests.coverage_risk_exec_adaptation: backtest risk simulation + execution simulation covered by tests/test_backtest.py; the full Phase 6 risk/exec modules are not implemented yet (adaptation is later).
tests.mypy: ok (no issues found in 66 source files)

assumptions_and_decisions: docs/decisions/0005-phase-4-backtest-engine.md (deterministic reference strategy+series as the engine self-test until Phase 5 ships real candidates; structural next-bar-open look-ahead prevention; cost models tied to VERIFIED metadata with multipliers as the only stress knobs; risk simulation deliberately under-promises portfolio heat/net-beta until the Phase 6 risk manager; notional-train walk-forward with a once-only locked hold-out; config-pinned backtest_version + content-addressed indexed runs; capital-agnostic reporting).
known_gaps_risks: The reference strategy and series are a deterministic offline self-test fixture with a PLANTED causal edge - they exercise and prove the engine/walk-forward/stress machinery and gates, not a real tradable edge. Real research candidates (Section 12 families) flow through the identical engine in Phase 5.

Risk simulation enforces per-trade sizing, leverage-as-consequence and the min-notional/lot-step gate; portfolio heat, net-beta-to-BTC and correlation caps are deferred to the Phase 6 risk manager (RISK gate) rather than faked.

Spread/impact slippage uses modelled spread samples from the reference fixture and a configurable impact_coeff (0 by default); a real venue swaps in measured spreads/liquidity behind the same SlippageModel.

Per-run reports/backtest/ dumps are git-ignored (regenerable from configs/backtest.yaml + the deterministic fixture); committed provenance is the backtest_runs index row + completion/orchestrator reports.
next_step: Hand off to Reviewer Agent (re-run BT/WF/FEE/SLIP); do not begin Phase 5.
